summary: Celestial meadow zone tied to moon proximity and gathering rituals.
Large inland sea bordered by multiple houses and home to floating cities.
Dense mystical southern forest near Cosmouth.
Frozen storm-laden northern ocean with seasonal navigation limits.

Rural labor and supply belt of plains, workshops, and market towns where regional leaders, syndicate stewards, and patron-families manage domestic order beneath Soreheim Proper’s quotas and alliance law.
Treacherous northern cold terrain shaping travel and conflict.
Corrupted meadow of elven observation ruins and celestial manifestations.
Treacherous northern sea of whirlpools and frigid winds.
Harsh hierarchical tower-belt of massive city-towers where alliance governance, foreign affairs, industrial output, war production, and high-order manufacturing converge.
Storm-ridden southern sea connecting Psanan trade routes.

Warm trade-and-port belt built across the former Mal sphere, whose main population areas are highly industrialized and urban, where guild governance, warehousing, arbitration halls, and converted finance districts structure daily life.
Subterranean cave network tied to celestial glyph corruption and rare flora.
Eerie dune region of shifting sounds, glass, and ancient-message lore.
Union parity-completion locality focused on arbiters row.
Central fortified seat of Psanan where Great House rule, forge prestige, and civic renewal ceremony visibly converge.
Fortified quarry-settlement where ore extraction, partial refinement, predator defense, and hard clan-family patronage dominate daily survival.
Psanan ash-port where trade survives between cooling windows, soot tides, and public renewal display.
Largest Sheresh dome commune, where survival administration, celestial study, and public endurance ritual converge.
Shared anchorage where house prestige, shipping, and negotiated access intersect.
An irrigation-market choke point where grain, labor, and reserve control become political theater.
Prestigious rebuilt quarter known for surviving destruction, swearing public vows, and turning fire damage into civic memory.
Floating intellectual and shipwright metropolis of Cosmouth.
Highly urbanized workshop-and-archive district where artisans, copyists, and licensed magical trade professionals converge inside a dense industrial production zone.
Fortified hot-edge outpost where route captains, water-ration families, and predator-watch crews hold the desertward lanes of the Unternal supply belt.
Roadwarden service outpost focused on repairs, barracks life, and patrol support.
A forge-centered town built on older burn layers where prestige comes from making durable goods from former ruin.
Geothermal labor haven where recovering crews, repair workers, and shift-boss families regroup between tower-expansion and quarry assignments.
Agricultural and artisanal town supporting Shelkopolis while sitting near active glyph-cave pressure.
Sheresh research commune devoted to contamination measurement, cold-light study, and shield experimentation.
Union civic core of arbitration halls, tariff boards, bonded depots, and merchant assemblies set within a highly urbanized industrial successor center built over the former Mal trade-governance core.
Cultural and market town where harvest patron-families, quota clerks, and festival brokers turn field output into alliance redistribution.
Granary and bargaining hub where crop security, measure law, and caravan timing matter as much as soil.
A marginal Sheresh commune surviving around unstable heat coils and strict ration discipline.
Judicial-protocol district where house claims, maritime incidents, and status injuries are bound into settlement.
Storm-hardened floating fabrication quarter where emergency repair and naval improvisation define prestige.
Fortified administrative heart of Roaz, where legal identity, industry, and enforcement culture visibly merge.
Administrative-magical intake district where strange incidents are converted into casework.
Union parity-completion locality focused on ledger canal.
Traffic-heavy urban transshipment junction linking road caravans, river movement, and guild-monitored warehousing through dense freight staging and relay infrastructure.
Public ceremonial district where masks, banners, dance terraces, and civic braziers keep renewal visible in urban life.
Union parity-completion locality focused on mediators court.
Afterlife-service and divine mediation metropolis.
Northern road outpost guarding and servicing caravans between Shelkopolis and Fairhaven.
Underworld-edge outpost where descent crews, extraction teams, and House-backed hazard discipline meet.
Deliberative meeting quarter where confederation decisions are translated into practical obligations.
A reserve-accounting district where the principality converts harvest certainty into discipline and leverage.
Largest metropolis of Shelk and one of the major urban engines of the Principalities, defined by fashion, trade, refined civic order, and visible devotional plurality.
Remote Roazian coastal refuge and naval defense node where antimagic caution and quiet discipline are palpable.
Harsh hierarchical tower-centered production core where alliance governance, foreign affairs, war output, and high-order manufacturing converge across many Titan Towers.
A high-risk Sheresh commune built around sky observation, long-range signaling, and expedition launch cycles.
Rural crossroads market town where tools, textiles, grain contracts, and labor obligations move through workshop syndicates and patron-families.
A supervised intellectual market where copied texts, licenses, and disputes are priced in public.
Archive-harbor registrar district balancing maritime law, vessel identity, and salvage claims.
Dense urban market-quarter and negotiated exchange ground where guild representatives settle trade and visibility disputes amid stacked commerce, inspection lanes, and constant freight turnover.
A transit-facing funerary district where bodies, vows, and memory obligations cross from grief into procedure.
Sheresh crossing commune that keeps frozen route traffic, emergency shelter, and arbitration moving between domes.
A magically supervised records quarter where testimony, anomaly review, and evidentiary custody converge.
Refined terraced noble district of mansions, fountains, and elite culture.
Storehouse district where the politics of plenty become queues, weights, and storage rights.
Administrative-industrial ward where Roazian law, permits, prisoner labor accounting, and anti-magic inspection visibly meet.
Rougher labor and sellsword quarter of affordable goods, repair shops, and low-end taverns.
Public district of offerings, coffin contracts, and afterlife preparation where devotion and transaction remain visibly intertwined.
Scholarly district where book tariffs, copying labor, and licensed magical study become visibly commercial.
The industrious artisan and guild district of Shelkopolis.

Public text-control hall where copied works are inspected, taxed, and licensed.
Rough clientele and dangerous-contract meeting spot.

A natural consequence of contamination pressure and controlled entry into dome systems.
Valid because Soreheim agriculture and communal ownership require buffer infrastructure against drought, spoilage, and quota shock.

Beacon tower chain responsible for alarm transmission, route visibility, and night hazard posture.

More Psanan rather than different Psanan: a direct coastal expression of weapon export and volcanic materials trade.
A natural consequence of dome society needing a specialized repair-and-material node.
Valid because Soreheim already depends on storm-exposed industrial maintenance in the Eternal Lands; this is a downward maintenance layer, not new polity identity.

summary: More Psanan rather than different Psanan: a named internal industrial corridor for concentrated production.
A natural consequence of energy systems being too important to remain generic background.
High-output communal grain belt whose administration matters as much as its soil.
Union civic core of arbitration halls, tariff boards, bonded depots, and merchant assemblies set within a highly urbanized industrial successor center built over the former Mal trade-governance core.
Cultural and market town where harvest patron-families, quota clerks, and festival brokers turn field output into alliance redistribution.

A natural consequence of aurora study, mutation adaptation, and edge settlement survivability.

summary: Reflective planning site where long-horizon decisions and elite review are ritualized.
A controlled descent frontier for rare materials, danger claims, and licensed salvage.
Ambition-focused ceremonial office that binds labor achievement to moral legitimacy.
More Psanan rather than different Psanan: a route bureaucracy implied by repeated extraction movement.
A natural consequence of ration order, water capture, and survival stockpiling.
Harsh hierarchical tower-centered production core where alliance governance, foreign affairs, war output, and high-order manufacturing converge across many Titan Towers.
Central receiving and redistribution node for ore, tools, machine parts, and work orders.
Valid because the Eternal Lands already sustain large communal agriculture; this merely names the bureaucracy needed to manage it.
Rural crossroads market town where tools, textiles, grain contracts, and labor obligations move through workshop syndicates and patron-families.

Network of massive city-towers distributed across Soreheim Proper, with forge-bases, workshop middles, observatory and governance crowns, and outward development pressure pushing tower-linked industry into the Eternal Lands.
Valid because Titan Towers and interregional command imply relay offices rather than purely physical grandeur.

summary: Dense urban market-quarter and negotiated exchange ground where guild representatives settle trade and visibility disputes amid stacked commerce, inspection lanes, and constant freight turnover.
A natural consequence of route hazard and rescue logic across the polar zone.
A processional route of ash-patterned mosaics linking plazas, dedication halls, and memorial courts.
A ceremonial ring of basalt doorframes leading nowhere visible. Husbands returning with gifts sometimes stop here first to decide which truths can survive the threshold.
A natural bridge of pale mineral growth arcing over a contamination trench. Children dare each other to cross at dusk when the aurora makes the span look alive.

A frozen plain where pollution-stiffened blossoms grow in translucent clusters that chime before stormfront spell-surges. Gatherers, exile-runners, and wardens all want the field for different reasons.
A radiant contamination band where harvested light is taxed, painted, and sometimes stolen before it can be rendered safe.
Black basins below slag channels where exhausted crews leave tokens, bone charms, and wage marks before descending into heatworks that regularly fail quota.
A ridge of fused stone scored by repeated territorial clashes between trained behirs and anything too proud to detour. Prospectors read the grooves like weather.
A dead-silent salt basin that reflects torchlight seconds before torches are raised. Prison labor crews avoid it after dusk because voices seem to arrive from ahead of their speakers.
Wind-flayed blue crystal flats used to study contamination spread and exposure thresholds.
A black reed marsh fed by pulp runoff, lamp oil, and discarded copying fluids from tariff houses upriver. Waterproof pages drift through the muck with half-finished annotations still legible.
A suspended ruin beneath an abandoned span where discarded work camps became a black market village. Everyone there smiles like they know what your tools are worth.
A half-maintained bridge grid slung between tower works where signal lamps swing on chains over dizzying drops. Freight crews call it efficient; widows call it a place that eats promises.
A singing crevasse that harmonizes with nearby spellcasting and makes novice cantrip users dangerously brave. Nearby communes post listeners before they post guards.
Steam fissures cut through blue ice and turn every spoken word into layered echoes. Pilgrims come to test omens here, but smugglers use the noise to hide movement between communes.
A field of ash funnels that inhale sound before spitting it out miles away. Stone giants use them to pass warnings; raiders use them to stage fear.
Dry grassland crossed by abandoned wheel-ruts that reappear after rain. Caravan children say the land remembers every bargain made on it.
Subterranean Sheresh research vault holding archives, devices, and containment work beneath a major commune.
A reef shelf where wreckage drifts back against the tide days after storms. Cosmouth factors quietly pay for anything bearing intact seals or ledgers.
A heat-scarred trench where unfinished rail and old slag channels meet. Production guilds fight quietly over who gets to call it infrastructure and who gets blamed when it erupts.
A rotating bridge-joint the size of a district where fire-giant crews, dwarf riveters, and quota clerks all claim final authority over shutdown timing.
A hazardous frozen crossing where route wardens mark viable passage windows and many expeditions disappear.
A snow-buried stone road that hums old choir notes through the frost whenever travelers carry active spellwork across it. Couriers use the hum to count distance; predators use it to count prey.
Ancient ice formations humming as possible wards or beacons.
An inland orchard where wind-hardened fruit grows on trees bent into execution-shape by old storms. Families picnic nearby anyway during hardness festivals.
Icy crevices with concealed glyph markings and mutated creatures.
A low basin where failed protective fields left the snow glittering with translucent organo-crystalline dust. Hunters wear mirrored scarves and do not stay after sunset.
A ritual shoreline where rejected gifts, storm-thrown salvage, and family honor are sorted in public view.
Ancient glowing stone structures central to understanding Shelk glyph corruption.
Low, wind-combed fields that look peaceful until hidden drainage ditches, livestock drovers, and tithe patrols turn every crossing into a question of permission and yield.
A yard of anchor chains, giant bone markers, and purchased-afterlife wagons waiting for blessing windows.
Knife-grass and iron-bright stone stand in wind-bent rows across a testing ground used for wager duels, beast proofing, and public vows of return.
An orchard-court where household disputes are cooled through gift tasting, witness testimony, and controlled public humiliation. Outsiders often mistake the laughter for safety.
Partially failed emergency shelter on an intermittent route, still used in desperation.
A marsh route used by caravan guides who value silence, misdirection, and waterlogged caches over straight roads.
A blackwater causeway where tariffs, copies, and debts seem to cling to travelers longer than mud.
Trade crossroads prone to ambushes and glyph-affected faction pressure.
A neutral encampment circle marked by layered wagon ruts and shrine posts from six cultures. Deals made here are fair only if everyone is equally tired.
A vertical well of black-blue ice said to descend toward older contamination seams and older truths about the skies. Scholars debate it; smugglers map it.
Hidden cave grove of glowing celestial flora used in potions and enchantment.
A ford where devotional cairns and broken spearheads mark every crossing, making secrecy harder than danger.
A terraced quarry stained red by mineral runoff and old labor deaths. The lower steps still produce superior stone at a social price nobody agrees to count.
An abandoned roadwarden gatehouse whose wardrobe vault still holds rotting bright plumes and sealed travel logs. Patrols deny using it, which is how everyone knows they still do.
A ravine lined with old war banners turned to mineral husks. When war-law stories rise again, the ravine fills with listeners pretending they just came for shade.
A red-sand crossing marked by doorway shrines where husbands returning from campaign leave blood-pricked gifts before they are judged fit to re-enter household law.
A chain of rock cisterns that turn certain algae blooms pink after hard storms. Travelers prize the water; locals prize controlling who gets to call it communal.
An old road that forks where it should not, lined by red feathers that appear honest until the wind changes.
Major Psanan civic square for ember dances, masked processionals, and public renewal observance.
A broken string of devotional anchor-stones where caravans-by-sea pay tribute before risking whirlpool waters. Nomdaran brokers meet leviathan intermediaries here under strict gift protocol.
A stepped cataract where memorial goods snag in natural stone shelves below prayer paths. Pilgrims insist the water reveals what mourners tried not to admit.
A winding ascent where incense chimneys, old kill-zones, and maintenance shrines overlap in the same stone throat.
Lava-filled underworld tunnels containing rare minerals, relic pressure, and descent danger.
A high plain market-stop where trade promises are sung into lacquered ribbon tallies before the caravan moves on. Break the ribbon and three regions hear about it by dusk.
Black steps descending into a tide cave where afterlife purchases, memorial processions, and coffin-barges converge under priests who never forget a debt.
A stretch of road where stamped books and confiscated copies are sometimes found stacked in perfect dry piles after rain. No border clerk admits sending them there.
Corrupted elven monoliths and observation ruins pulsing with celestial glyph energy.
Central stone platform radiating strong celestial energy and defending enforcer manifestations.
A broken imperial road fused into glass ribs by repeated storm strikes. Couriers still risk it because the old route cuts hours from travel when the sky is willing.
Half-frozen sculpture grounds where brass bells grow through the ice from older civic works. The tones calm some mutations and worsen others.
Heat-sunk extraction pits where mineral slurries, monster bone ash, and runoff from old forge districts settle into pale layers prized by armorers and grave-tenders alike.
A softbank where funeral offerings and political apologies both wash ashore. Priests, thieves, and grieving families all arrive before dawn for different reasons.
An oath court complex where rebuilding vows are sworn before civic witnesses and recorded in stone.
A sequence of buoy shrines and weighted prayer cables marking the safest imperfect way through hostile water. Every captain claims to know the real pattern.
A snowfield studded with long-buried lantern masts whose glow wakes during contamination surges. Route wardens use the color drift to judge whether it is safe to continue.
Tall reedwater where floodgrain caravans vanish one axle at a time. The fen keeps footprints poorly but keeps gossip very well.
A salt-white road raised over brine flats where returning husbands leave gifts at household markers and rival matriarchies quietly measure one another's losses.
A wagon re-routing field where caravans are quietly reassigned under storm lantern code. Teamsters say no route is truly lost here, only sold twice.
